business_description.nature_of_business: Commercial construction/building contractor (carpentry, electrical, plumbing, masonry, painting, project management)

coverages_discussed.notes: Experience modification factor of 0.86 effective 08/01/2025; Large Risk rating plan; Risk ID 609402-1243

positives: Experience modification factor below 1.00 (0.86), indicating better-than-average loss experience
Actual excess losses ($212,733) significantly below expected excess losses ($1,132,496)
Large Risk rating plan qualification indicating substantial premium size
concerns: 20 total claims across the three-year experience period
10 claims in policy year 2021-2022 representing the highest claim frequency
Primary losses of $335,671 incurred during experience period
risk_highlights.loss_history_summary: Three-year experience period (2021-2024) with 20 total claims, $546,531 total paid, $548,454 total incurred, $335,671 primary losses, and $212,733 excess losses. Claim counts by year: 2023-2024 (7 claims), 2022-2023 (3 claims), 2021-2022 (10 claims).

extracted_text_sections.executive_summary: PCRB Experience Rating Worksheet for Keystone Commercial Builders, Inc. calculating a Final Experience Modification Factor of 0.86 effective 08/01/2025 through 08/01/2026. Total payroll $31,094,800 across seven class codes with expected losses of $901,952.
extracted_text_sections.operations_description: Construction operations across multiple trades: Carpentry NOC (Class 5403), Electrical Wiring (5190), Plumbing NOC (5183), Masonry NOC (5022), Interior Painting (5474), Project Manager/Superintendent (5606), and Clerical Office Employees NOC (8810).
extracted_text_sections.claims_narrative: Experience rating calculation: Actual Primary Losses (Ap) $335,671; Actual Excess Losses (Ae) $212,733; Expected Excess Losses (Ee) $1,132,496; Ballast Value (B) $13,748; Weighting Value (W) 0.574; Expected Losses (E) $1,095,520. Numerator $953,971 / Denominator $1,109,268 = Computed Modification Factor 0.8600. Approved 03/15/2026 by Analyst ID RA-9405.
